intro: Carduncellus rhiphaeus, also known as Rampion or Wild Rampion, is a perennial plant belonging to the Campanulaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating various digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Carduncellus rhiphaeus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
